basename.summary: Extracts the trailing name component (file or directory name) from <path> and stores it in <strvar>. The path need not exist.

dirname.summary: Removes the filename portion of <path> and stores the resulting directory path in <strvar>. The path need not exist.

fileclose.summary: Closes the file specified by <file handle>. The handle is no longer valid after this command.

fileconcat.summary: Appends a copy of <file2> to the end of <file1>; the two files must not be the same. Sets system variable result to 0 on success, non-zero on failure.

filecopy.summary: Copies <file1> to <file2>, overwriting <file2> if it exists; the files must differ. Sets system variable result to 0 on success, non-zero on failure.

filecreate.summary: Creates and opens <filename>, truncating it to zero if it exists, with the pointer at the start. Stores a positive integer handle in <file handle> on success or -1 on failure, and sets result to 0 on success or non-zero on failure.

filedelete.summary: Deletes the file specified by <filename>. Sets system variable result to 0 on success, non-zero on failure.

filelock.summary: Acquires an exclusive lock on the file for the calling process. <timeout> (seconds) controls waiting: 0 returns immediately, omitted waits forever. Sets result to 0 on success, 1 on failure.

filemarkptr.summary: Marks the current file pointer of the open file specified by <file handle>. The marked position can be recalled with the fileseekback command.

fileopen.summary: Opens <filename> in binary mode, creating it if absent. Stores the handle in <file handle> on success or -1 on failure. <append flag> nonzero puts the pointer at the end; <readonly flag> nonzero opens read-only.

fileread.summary: Reads <read byte> bytes (1-511) from the open file into <strvar>, advancing the pointer. Sets result to 1 if end of file is reached, otherwise 0.

filereadln.summary: Reads a line from the open file into <strvar>, advancing the pointer to the next line. Sets result to 1 if end of file is reached during the read, otherwise 0.

filerename.summary: Renames <file1> to <file2>; the two names must not be the same. Sets system variable result to 0 on success, non-zero on failure.

filesearch.summary: Searches for the file or folder specified by <filename>. Sets system variable result to 1 if found, 0 if not found.

fileseek.summary: Moves the file pointer by <offset> from a position set by <origin>: 0 = beginning of file, 1 = current position, 2 = end of file. Offset may be positive or negative.

fileseekback.summary: Moves the file pointer of the open file to the position previously marked by the filemarkptr command.

filestat.summary: Retrieves information about a file or directory: stores its size in bytes in <size>, last modification time in <mtime>, and drive number in <drive>. Sets result to -1 on error.

filestrseek.summary: Searches forward from the current pointer in the open file for <string>. If found, moves the pointer just after the string and sets result to 1; otherwise leaves the pointer and sets result to 0.

filestrseek2.summary: Searches backward from the current pointer in the open file for <string>. If found, moves the pointer to just before the string and sets result to 1; otherwise leaves the pointer and sets result to 0.

filetruncate.summary: Resizes <filename> to <size> bytes, padding with null characters when extending. Sets system variable result to 0 on success, -1 on error.

fileunlock.summary: Unlocks the whole file, enabling other processes to access it. Sets system variable result to 0 on success, 1 on failure.

filewrite.summary: Writes <data> to the file specified by <file handle>. A string is written as-is; an integer is treated as an ASCII code (0-255) and written as the corresponding character.

filewriteln.summary: Writes <data> followed by newline characters (CR+LF) to the file. A string is written as-is; an integer is treated as an ASCII code (0-255) and written as the corresponding character.

findfirst.summary: Finds the first file matching the <file name> pattern. On success stores a directory handle in <dir handle>, the found filename in <strvar>, and sets result to 1; on failure sets the handle to -1, <strvar> to empty, and result to 0.

findnext.summary: Finds the next file matching the pattern, using the handle from findfirst. Stores the next filename in <strvar> and sets result to 1, or clears <strvar> and sets result to 0 when no more matches exist.

findclose.summary: Releases the directory handle obtained from findfirst. Should be called after file searching is complete.

foldercreate.summary: Creates a new folder named <foldername>. Sets system variable result to 0 on success, non-zero on failure.

folderdelete.summary: Deletes the folder specified by <foldername>; it cannot delete a folder containing files. Sets system variable result to 0 on success, non-zero on failure.

foldersearch.summary: Searches for the folder specified by <foldername>. Sets result to 1 if it is a folder, 0 otherwise (including for a file), so it cannot reliably check folder existence.

getdir.summary: Stores MACRO's current working directory in <strvar>. The path ends with a backslash only for a root directory.

getfileattr.summary: Retrieves the attributes of <filename> and stores them in system variable result as bitwise flags (e.g. $1 read-only, $10 directory, $20 archive), or -1 on failure.

makepath.summary: Builds a full path from directory <dir> and file name <name>, inserting a backslash separator as needed, and stores the result in <strvar>.

setdir.summary: Changes MACRO's current working directory to <dir>, affecting subsequent commands like fileopen. This differs from changedir, which sets Tera Term's file transfer directory.

setfileattr.summary: Sets the attributes of <filename> to the combined bitwise <attributes> (e.g. read-only, hidden, archive). Sets system variable result to 1 on success, 0 on failure.
